Secure Communication App

End-to-end encrypted chat built with Flutter & Node.js

A cross-platform mobile messaging application that ensures complete privacy through end-to-end encryption. The client is built in React Native, while the backend is a Node.js + Express server. Real-time messaging is powered by WebSocket (Socket.IO), and persistent storage uses MongoDB.

  • 🔑 Key Exchange: RSA-2048 asymmetric handshake
  • 🔒 Message Encryption: AES-256-GCM per session
  • 💾 Secure Storage: Secure Storage for private keys
  • ⚡ Real-Time: Socket.IO WebSockets
  • 🗄️ Database: MongoDB with Mongoose ODM

Reactnative Node.js Express MongoDB Socket.IO

View source and detailed documentation on GitHub: github.com/joel-carlson/SecureMessagingApp

Invasive Species Reporting App

Aug 2023 – May 2024 · University of Colorado Boulder

A cross-platform mobile application built to help the Gunnison community combat the spread of Cheatgrass and other invasive species through crowd-sourced field reports. Users submit geo-tagged sightings that are aggregated and visualized for researchers and land managers.

  • 📱 Cross-platform: React Native interface for iOS and Android
  • 🗺️ Offline maps: Interactive offline maps for use in remote areas without connectivity
  • ☁️ Cloud backend: Node.js REST API backed by AWS DynamoDB for scalable data management
  • 📊 Crowd-sourced: User-generated submissions for tracking and mapping invasive species spread
  • 👥 Team: Collaborative development with tight deadlines and limited budget

React Native Node.js AWS DynamoDB RESTful API

Battle of Wits: Examining the Deceitful and Trustworthy Behaviors of LLMs

Apr 2025 - Apr 2025 | University of Colorado Boulder

In Battle of Wits, we introduce a two-agent social-deduction game, inspired by The Princess Bride, to probe when and how large language models (LLMs) choose to trust or deceive, and to uncover the reasoning behind those decisions. We repurpose a “cups of poison” setup into a controlled “boxes and block” scenario and evaluate a 7 billion-parameter model (gemma:7b) under zero-, one-, and two-shot prompting. By assigning explicit truth-telling or deceitful roles to each agent, we measure block-location guessing accuracy across the 2×2 belief–disposition matrix.

  • 🤖 Deceit Conditions: Deceit emerges reliably only when prompt examples and deceptive intent align, with one- and two-shot prompts significantly boosting the defender’s ability to mislead.
  • 🔍 Mixed Responses: Even with a deceitful mandate, LLMs sometimes default to truthful or mixed responses, yielding near-50 % baseline accuracy.
  • ❓ Zero-Shot Insight: Zero-shot prompting exhibits an inverse relationship between question count and guess accuracy, suggesting that simpler interrogation can outperform more elaborate questioning without examples.

Ollama Python Jupyter PyTorch Hugging Face

This work delivers a reproducible framework for quantifying LLMs’ social-behavior biases, laying critical groundwork for safely integrating autonomous agents into trust-sensitive, high-stakes environments, and offering deep insights into the models’ decision-making processes.

github.com/MilesMena/battle_of_wits

Python-Based AI Learning Agents for Atari Games

Apr 2023 · University of Colorado Boulder

Implemented and compared three reinforcement learning algorithms — Q-learning, Approximate Q-learning, and Deep Q-Network (DQN) — by training agents to play classic Atari games. The project involved training, evaluation, and iterative optimization across multiple game environments to study how different learning paradigms converge.

  • 🎮 Algorithms: Q-learning, Approximate Q-learning, and Deep Q-Network (DQN) side-by-side
  • 📈 Analysis: Comprehensive performance benchmarking across multiple Atari environments
  • ⚙️ Optimization: Hyperparameter tuning and reward shaping to improve agent convergence speed
  • 📊 Evaluation: Data analysis and visualization to measure and compare agent decision-making

Python TensorFlow OpenAI Gym Pandas Reinforcement Learning

Memory Storage Implementation in C

Oct 2021 – Nov 2021 · University of Colorado Boulder

Designed and implemented a complete memory management system in C, integrating cache, virtual memory, and main memory into a unified hierarchy. The system demonstrates the full memory stack and optimizes access patterns through strategic use of efficient algorithms and system-level programming techniques.

  • 🗄️ Memory Hierarchy: Unified cache, virtual memory, and main memory subsystems
  • ⚡ Optimization: Algorithmic strategies to minimize cache misses and reduce access latency
  • 🔧 Systems Programming: Low-level C with manual memory management and pointer arithmetic
  • 📐 Architecture: Modular design enabling independent testing of each memory tier

C Memory Management Systems Programming Algorithm Design

Unix System Programming in C++ and Python

Jan 2021 – Apr 2021 · University of Colorado Boulder

Built a custom Unix shell environment implementing core OS concepts from scratch in C++ and Python. The project covers the full lifecycle of process and resource management, demonstrating a deep understanding of how modern operating systems work under the hood.

  • 🔀 Process Management: Custom fork() and exec() for process spawning and replacement
  • 📁 I/O Redirection: File redirection using dup() / dup2() for stdin/stdout/stderr piping
  • 🧵 Concurrency: Multi-threaded execution with mutex locks and semaphores for synchronization
  • ⚙️ Shell Commands: Custom implementations of common Unix utilities with pipeline chaining

C++ Python Multithreading POSIX

DeepGlobe Challenge 2 with Transformers

Apr 2025 – Present · University of Colorado Boulder

Extending the DeepGlobe Satellite Image Understanding Challenge (2nd Competition) by evaluating SAM 2.1 as a modern alternative to the U-Net–based architectures that won the original contest. This work investigates whether large-scale vision transformers can surpass specialized convolutional models on satellite imagery tasks, paving the way for more versatile remote-sensing tools.

  • 🛰️ Task: Land-cover segmentation of high-resolution satellite imagery across 6 terrain classes
  • 📐 Benchmarking: IoU, F1 score, and inference speed compared against top U-Net variants
  • 🔍 Feature analysis: Transformer-driven representations for improved boundary delineation in complex scenes
  • 🔄 Reproducibility: Automated preprocessing and augmentation pipeline for fair, reproducible comparisons

Python PyTorch SAM 2.1 Computer Vision Transformers

github.com/joel-carlson/DeepGlobe-2-Transformers

Coin Flip Betting Game

Apr 2023 – May 2023 · University of Colorado Boulder

A Java-based multi-device betting game backed by a centrally hosted database. Players create accounts and place bets on the outcome of a coin toss — heads or tails — with real-time results and persistent account balances. Built with a focus on concurrency, database design, and networked client-server communication.

  • 🪙 Gameplay: Multi-player betting on coin toss outcomes with account management
  • 🗄️ Database: Centrally hosted database for persistent user accounts and bet history
  • 🔗 Networked: Client-server architecture supporting multiple simultaneous devices
  • ⚡ Agile: Developed iteratively using Agile methodology with sprint-based delivery

Java Database Design Agile Client-Server

github.com/JTxCo/412-FinalProj-CoinFlip

Professional Experience

Machine Learning Research Intern

Rocky Mountain Biological Laboratory
Summer 2023
  • Developed CNN models to classify plant species with 92% accuracy.
  • Automated data-cleaning pipeline in Python, reducing prep time by 40%.

Software Engineering Intern

BioMEMS
Fall 2022
  • Built RESTful APIs in Node.js, serving 2,000+ daily requests.
  • Integrated OAuth2 authentication, improving security.
ML Research Intern @ RMBL (Summer 2023)

Overview

Collaborated with ecologists to build a CNN-based pipeline classifying alpine plants from field images.

Code Snippet


import torchvision.models as models
import torch.nn as nn

class PlantClassifier(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_classes=12):
        super().__init__()
        self.backbone = models.resnet18(pretrained=True)
        self.backbone.fc = nn.Linear(512, num_classes)

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.backbone(x)

Deep Dive

  • Labeled 5,000+ images with a team of 4 biologists.
  • Hyperparameter tuning in TensorFlow → +6% accuracy.
  • Packaged as Flask app with REST endpoint for field use.
Software Engineering Intern @ BioMEMS (Fall 2022)

Overview

Designed and deployed RESTful services + a real-time dashboard for biomedical sensors.

Code Snippet


const express = require('express');
const app = express();

// OAuth2 middleware
app.use(require('./middleware/oauth2'));

app.get('/api/data', (req, res) => {
  // fetch sensor readings...
  res.json({ data: readings });
});

app.listen(3000, () => console.log('API running'));

Deep Dive

  • Designed PostgreSQL schema; wrote migration scripts.
  • Built React dashboard with real-time WebSocket updates.
  • Achieved test coverage from 55% → 85% via Jest.

Meet Joel Carlson

Joel Carlson

ML Engineer & Master's Student

Master's student in Computer Science at CU Boulder, specializing in AI and Machine Learning. My work spans computer vision, reinforcement learning, and LLM behavior research. When I'm not training models, I'm contributing to open-source projects and writing about AI on Medium.

I'm currently seeking full-time ML engineering and software engineering roles. Feel free to reach out!
